diary:
Woke up in Bothell to that early-June sunny hush, did my quick rooftop herb check and watered everything like it was part of my tuning routine. Breakfast was light—just enough to get my head straight—then I headed out while the streets were still awake. The drive to the studio in Issaquah felt unusually calm, like the city had decided to behave before the day got loud.

I spent the morning in the tracking room, calibrating levels and chasing little weird frequencies that only show up when you’re looking for them. By lunch I wandered over toward Pike Place Market, grabbed something quick, and let the crowd noise reset my ears. On the way back, I kept thinking about how the light rail closure would funnel people toward traffic—sure enough, the afternoon commute had that extra pulse.

After wrapping up editing and doing a final playback check, I swung by QFC in Bellevue for groceries, then hit the Seattle Athletic Club for a short strength session. Later at The Pike Brewing Company in Pioneer Square, I watched USA vs Wales highlights with my friend—half cheering, half laughing at how intense everyone gets. I’m tired, but in a good, settled way.
thought_process: I tried to keep the day “clean” in my head, starting with the rooftop watering and a calm drive—if I’m relaxed early, the session work usually clicks faster. In the studio I chose to focus on calibration and stem organization rather than chasing creative tweaks; today felt more about setting a solid technical baseline before anything else. The light rail closure between Westlake and University District nudged my timing, so I didn’t assume downtown would be easy—traffic felt busier, and I adjusted without stressing. The World Cup match airing at 11am PDT definitely shifted the atmosphere downtown later; even when I wasn’t watching live, I could feel the early bar energy. My mood stayed steady through the afternoon, then warmed up at night during the friend dinner and highlights—like the day finally exhaled.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
